items.title: BoE splits on rates outlook as it keeps borrowing costs on hold | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
Bank of England policymakers split over whether to cut interest rates. While the BoE kept its benchmark bank rate on hold at 4.75%, three out of nine of the members of the Monetary Policy Committee voted for a cut to 4.5%.

#News #Reuters #Newsfeed #boe #ratecut #europeanmarkets

items.title: Gisele Pelicot says she 'never regretted' making her trial public
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
Gisele Pelicot said she "never regretted" the decision to make her trial public, after a French court found her ex-husband Dominique Pelicot guilty on Dec. 19 of repeatedly drugging and raping her for almost a decade, and inviting dozens of strangers to rape her unconscious body.
 
All the Frenchman's 50 co-defendants were also found guilty of rape, attempted rape or sexual assault.
 
Dominique Pelicot, who was married to Gisele for 50 years, had pleaded guilty to the charges and a panel of five judges sentenced him to the maximum 20 years in jail, as requested by prosecutors.
 
#Reuters #GiselePelicot #France #trial #DominiquePelicot

items.title: Macro Matters: What will 2025 have in store for markets? | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
A new US president, a trade war and political upheaval in Europe’s powerhouses are keeping investors on guard heading into 2025. ING's Carsten Brzeski tells @Reuters why inflation will remain one of the key risks in the year ahead.
 
#News #Reuters #Newsfeed #markets #2025 #newyear

items.title: Luigi Mangione, suspect in CEO killing, faces federal murder charge
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of UnitedHealth executive Brian Thompson, is being charged with federal murder and stalking crimes, according to a court document, alongside state murder and terrorism charges previously announced by New York prosecutors.

items.title: Market Insight: Trade wars 'are going to detract' from economic growth | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
Markets dropped this week when Fed policymakers cut their projections for rate cuts in 2025, forecasting strong growth and high inflation. EY chief economist Gregory Daco says tariffs imposed by the Trump administration could lead to a trade war that weakens growth around the world.
